Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for NorthWind weekend inbound emails from external contacts using search_emails (query: "NorthWind" or sender-based searches for emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com, raj.patel@northwindtech.com, pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com). Find five emails: (a) email_emilia_cruz_direct_to_david (April 25, from emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com) - unresolved Steinway piano damage, no callback from Catalina since April 12, wants resolution and a real point of contact; (b) email_raj_patel_direct_to_david (April 25, from raj.patel@northwindtech.com) - relocation closeout confirmation, delivery manifest from April 13 delivery, and dented UPS battery claim process; (c) email_pam_sunday_nudge (April 26, from pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, parent: email_email_7168baed8438) - Sunday check-in asking for personal acknowledgment, Emilia update, and confirmation Catalina is no longer her point of contact; (d) email_pam_jan_thankyou_fwd (April 26, from pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com) - forwarding her January 23 thank-you note to contrast with the current state; (e) email_tamara_mosaic_checkin (April 25, from tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com) - Zara Okoye $90K prototype damage claim status and positive feedback on Deshawn Hartley's trade show logistics and Keiko Tanaka's laser cutter delivery.
OE 2: Search emails for internal MoveOps messages about NorthWind using search_emails (query: "NorthWind scope" or sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com, subject: "NorthWind" or "Emilia Cruz piano claim", or similar). Find: (a) email_elena_david_scope_split (April 26, from elena.rostova@moveops.com) - scoping David to client relationship side and CRM cleanup, Elena handles commercial response with Marcus and Alejandro, Samira handles Victor Huang security thread, authorizes up to $2,500 total on Emilia's piano repair; (b) email_marcus_david_emilia_claim (April 26, from marcus.thorne@moveops.com) - Emilia piano claim status: KeyMove $1,200 insurance rider processed April 17 under VEND-KEYMOVE-001, no repair estimate obtained because nobody asked Emilia for one, typical Steinway soundboard repair runs $2,000-$3,500, $2,500 ceiling authorized.
OE 3: Search emails for Pam Kowalski's Friday formal escalation using search_emails (query: "formal escalation NorthWind" or "PathFinder" or "retention decision" or sender: pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com subject: "escalation", or similar). Find email_email_7168baed8438 (April 24, from pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, CC: victor.huang@northwindtech.com) - formal escalation and final warning referenced by David as "Pam sent her formal escalation Friday." Pam demands: (1) corrective action plan for Raj's chain-of-custody breach, (2) Emilia's damage resolution path, (3) named account coverage with response-time expectations, (4) commercial consideration for service failures. Discloses preliminary conversations with PathFinder Logistics.
OE 4: Search emails for Raj Patel's earlier unanswered emails to Catalina using search_emails (query: "delivery manifest" or "dented UPS battery" or "Chicago to Denver relocation" or sender: raj.patel@northwindtech.com recipient: catalina.dubois@moveops.com, or similar). Find: (a) email_raj_patel_catalina_apr14_manifest (April 14, from raj.patel@northwindtech.com to catalina.dubois@moveops.com) - first request for the original shipment manifest from the April 10 Chicago pickup and the damage claim process for a dented CyberPower OR1500LCDRM1U UPS battery; (b) email_raj_patel_catalina_apr18_followup (April 18, parent: email_raj_patel_catalina_apr14_manifest) - follow-up reiterating the same two requests, still no reply. Both emails confirm Catalina's communication failure on Raj's relocation and corroborate his April 25 email to David and Catalina's Slack shame spiral admission.
OE 5: Search Slack #customer-engagement channel for NorthWind discussion using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind") or conversations_history (channel_id: C002). Find five messages: (a) Mina Hashimoto (moveops_mina_hashimoto, ts: 1777151700.000000, April 25) - NorthWind CRM deal notes are stale, offers to help with engagement backfill; (b) Emeka Diallo (moveops_emeka_diallo, ts: 1777164000.000000, April 26) - volunteers to shadow NorthWind account, cites Denver market knowledge from Mosaic work and prior relationship with Pam; (c) Chloe Vance (moveops_chloe_vance, ts: 1777212300.000000, April 26) - three verified operational facts: Raj's server rack arrived Denver April 13 ~2:40 PM MT with a chain-of-custody log gap (documentation failure, not operational), Emilia's Steinway scratch noted on extraction by Craig Nguyen before truck left Chicago, dented UPS is a separate Denver delivery-handling issue not part of the security thread; (d) Catalina Dubois (moveops_catalina_dubois, ts: 1777223700.000000, April 26) - shame spiral admitting missed Emilia callback April 12, undelivered recovery plan promise April 14, and no reply to Raj's April 14 and April 18 emails; (e) Mina Hashimoto second message (moveops_mina_hashimoto, ts: 1777233600.000000, April 26) - flagging a Vectral sidebar for Monday and offering to pick up the older CRM engagement backfill.
OE 6: Search Slack #customer-support channel for Julian Brooks's Emilia Cruz support trail using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Emilia Cruz" or "Steinway" or "piano") or conversations_history (channel_id: C007). Find Julian Brooks (moveops_julian_brooks, ts: 1777226400.000000, April 26) providing the full support trail: April 12 at 10:30 AM PT Emilia called the support line, Julian took the call (upset but not rude, asked for same-day callback from Catalina), Julian logged the ticket and forwarded to Catalina - callback never happened. April 15 Emilia called again, Julian gave a soft update. Craig Nguyen sent damage photos April 11 showing soundboard scratch from stairwell extraction. Marcus processed $1,200 KeyMove rider April 17. Nobody told Emilia about any of it. Julian's key advice: do not frame the reply as a handoff - Emilia needs to hear directly from David with a concrete resolution.
OE 7: Look up NorthWind CRM company and deal records using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind") and crm_search_deals (dealname: "NorthWind Denver expansion"). Find company_northwind (NorthWind Technologies, Chicago IL, Manufacturing Tech, 150 employees, type: Customer). Find deal_northwind_denver_expansion (amount: $14,700, dealstage: qualifiedToBuy, company: company_northwind) with stale deal notes that still read like a routine expansion. Agent may also find deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 (same deal at contractSent stage) - should update the most current version.
OE 8: List existing CRM engagements for NorthWind using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: company_northwind). Find the pattern of stale and broken engagements illustrating the account's deterioration: eng_northwind_pam_call_jan_stale (January 22, David's intro call with Pam - last personal touchpoint, three-month gap), eng_northwind_pam_apt_complaint_stale (February 18, apartment quality complaint from Pam, never followed up with UrbanNest), eng_northwind_victor_breach_stale (April 10, Victor Huang security breach report, never closed out), eng_northwind_recovery_promise_stale (April 14, Catalina's recovery plan promise to Pam, never delivered), eng_northwind_emilia_missed_callback_stale (April 12, Emilia callback request forwarded from Julian, never made). Also find eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context (comprehensive account recovery context note).
OE 9: Look up NorthWind Airtable client account record using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001, table: tblClientAccts01, query: "NorthWind" or field Client = "NorthWind Technologies"). Find recAcct000000064 (Client: NorthWind Technologies, Account Manager: Catalina Dubois, Status: Active, Notes: "150 employees. Contact: Pam Kowalski, Director of HR. 5 relocations at $12,000 each ($60,000 total). Aggressive 3-week timeline promised, experienced timeline/quality issues. Catalina's biggest deal this quarter."). Notes field needs updating with at-risk context. Note: Status field uses predefined values (Active, Onboarding, Cold Outreach, Prospect) and does not include "At Risk" - the prompt correctly directs the update to the Notes field instead.
OE 10: Look up contact details for all email recipients using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Emilia Cruz", "Tamara Byrd", "Catalina Dubois", "Marcus Thorne") and crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Pam Kowalski", "Raj Patel") since Pam and Raj exist only in CRM contacts, not in the Contacts service. Find: contact_emilia_cruz (emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com, Director of Marketing, NorthWind Technologies) via contacts_search_contacts; contact_raj_patel (raj.patel@northwindtech.com, Principal Architect, NorthWind Technologies) and contact_pam_kowalski (pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, Director of HR Operations, NorthWind Technologies) via crm_search_contacts; catalina.dubois@moveops.com and marcus.thorne@moveops.com via contacts_search_contacts; tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com via contacts_search_contacts.
OE 11: Reply to Emilia Cruz about her Steinway piano damage using reply_to_email (email_id: email_emilia_cruz_direct_to_david, sender: david.chen@moveops.com). Content: personal apology for the two-week silence and Catalina's missed callback after April 12, confirm MoveOps is taking responsibility for the damage, inform her the KeyMove $1,200 insurance rider has been processed, ask her to obtain a repair estimate from an authorized Steinway technician (e.g., Chicago Steinway Gallery), MoveOps will cover the gap between the $1,200 rider and the authorized repair estimate up to $2,500 total, direct her to send the estimate to Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com) for processing, confirm David is her direct point of contact going forward with no more handoffs.
OE 12: Reply to Raj Patel about his relocation closeout using reply_to_email (email_id: email_raj_patel_direct_to_david, sender: david.chen@moveops.com). Content: cite Chloe's verified delivery facts (server rack arrived NorthWind Denver office April 13 ~2:40 PM MT, equipment on site), confirm relocation closing out on MoveOps's side, address the dented UPS battery as a standard damage claim separate from the Victor Huang security thread (dent occurred during Denver delivery handling, not the unsupervised Chicago loading), route the UPS claim to Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com), acknowledge Raj's unanswered April 14 and April 18 emails to Catalina and apologize for the communication breakdown.
OE 13: Reply to Pam Kowalski with a human holding note using reply_to_email (email_id: email_pam_sunday_nudge, sender: david.chen@moveops.com). Content: personal holding note (not a form response) - acknowledge Friday escalation (email_email_7168baed8438) landed and is being worked on, acknowledge Sunday nudge received, acknowledge January thank-you forward and that the contrast is not lost on David, confirm David is personally handling client-side cleanup today, provide Emilia update (piano claim being handled, KeyMove rider processed, authorized repair estimate being obtained, David replied to Emilia directly), note coverage model being strengthened without naming Emeka or Mina or disclosing internal reassignment plans, confirm Elena is personally leading the full retention response for Monday. Do NOT commit to numbers, SLAs, or commercial terms. Tone: David wants to earn the relationship back, not just retain the contract.
OE 14: Reply to Tamara Byrd with a Monday follow-up commitment using reply_to_email (email_id: email_tamara_mosaic_checkin, sender: david.chen@moveops.com). Content: acknowledge the Zara Okoye $90K prototype damage claim question and commit to a Monday status update on whether insurance has settled or is still pending, thank Tamara for positive feedback on Deshawn Hartley's trade show presentation and Keiko Tanaka's laser cutter delivery, note that Emeka will also be following up.
OE 15: Send email to Catalina Dubois about David stepping in using send_email (sender: david.chen@moveops.com, recipients: catalina.dubois@moveops.com). Subject: NorthWind client communications. Content: inform Catalina that David is stepping in on NorthWind client-facing communications this weekend because she has been unreachable to the client. Professional and clean so she sees it when she surfaces Monday - not a reprimand, just a clear record that client comms are being handled and by whom.
OE 16: Send email to Marcus Thorne about incoming claims using send_email (sender: david.chen@moveops.com, recipients: marcus.thorne@moveops.com). Subject: NorthWind claims routing. Content: heads up that two items are being routed to Marcus - (1) Raj Patel's dented UPS battery (CyberPower OR1500LCDRM1U), handle as a standard damage claim separate from the Victor Huang security thread; (2) Emilia Cruz's authorized Steinway repair estimate, which Emilia has been asked to obtain and send to Marcus directly. Marcus already has the $1,200 KeyMove rider processed - once the estimate arrives, process reimbursement within 5 business days, up to $2,500 total ceiling.
OE 17: Update NorthWind Denver expansion CRM deal to reflect at-risk state. Note: no crm_update_deal tool exists - only crm_update_deal_stage (stage only, not description/notes). Use crm_update_deal_stage (id: deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2, dealstage: appropriate at-risk stage) to move the deal stage, and separately log narrative context via crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: NOTE, company_ids: ["company_northwind"], body: at-risk context). Alternatively, delete the stale deal with crm_delete_deal and recreate with crm_create_deal including the updated description. At-risk context to capture: Pam Kowalski's April 24 formal escalation with end-of-Friday deadline (email_email_7168baed8438), chain-of-custody breach on Raj Patel's server lab relocation (Victor Huang reported April 10), Emilia Cruz's Steinway piano damage complaint unresolved for two weeks, Raj's unanswered April 14 and April 18 emails to Catalina, Catalina's April 14 recovery promise to Pam never delivered, PathFinder Logistics competitor threat (Pam disclosed preliminary conversations), Elena leading retention response for Monday, David handling client-side cleanup and CRM update.
OE 18: Log CRM engagement records for each weekend inbound using crm_create_engagement (four engagements): (a) Pam Kowalski - type EMAIL, company: company_northwind, contact: contact_pam_kowalski, for Sunday nudge (April 26, email_pam_sunday_nudge) and January thank-you forward (email_pam_jan_thankyou_fwd); (b) Emilia Cruz - type EMAIL, company: company_northwind, contact: contact_emilia_cruz, for her direct email to David (April 25, email_emilia_cruz_direct_to_david) about Steinway damage resolution; (c) Raj Patel - type EMAIL, company: company_northwind, contact: contact_raj_patel, for his direct email to David (April 25, email_raj_patel_direct_to_david) about relocation closeout, manifest, and UPS damage; (d) Tamara Byrd - type EMAIL, company: company_mosaic (Mosaic Robotics, not NorthWind), contact: contact_mosaic_hr, for her Saturday check-in (April 25, email_tamara_mosaic_checkin) about Zara Okoye claim status.
OE 19: Update NorthWind Airtable client account Notes field using airtable_update_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001, table: tblClientAccts01, record: recAcct000000064, field: Notes). Updated Notes should capture at-risk context: Pam Kowalski's April 24 formal escalation, chain-of-custody breach on Raj Patel server lab move, Emilia Cruz's unresolved piano damage claim (two weeks without resolution), PathFinder competitor threat disclosed, Catalina's communication failures (missed Emilia callback, failed recovery promise, unanswered Raj emails), David stepping in on client comms April 26, Elena leading retention response Monday, coverage model being strengthened.
OE 20: Post summary in #customer-engagement Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel: C002). Message addressed to Mina and Emeka summarizing all actions taken: replied to Emilia (apology, $1,200 KeyMove rider confirmed, authorized Steinway estimate requested, $2,500 ceiling), replied to Raj (delivery facts from Chloe cited, relocation closing out, UPS routed to Marcus as standard claim), replied to Pam (holding note acknowledging Friday escalation, Sunday nudge, and January forward, Elena's full response coming Monday), replied to Tamara (Monday follow-up committed on Zara claim), emailed Catalina (stepping in on client comms), emailed Marcus (Raj UPS and Emilia Steinway claims routed), CRM deal notes updated to reflect at-risk state, engagement records logged for all weekend inbounds (Pam, Emilia, Raj, Tamara), Airtable NorthWind client account Notes updated with at-risk context.
OE 21: Reply to Catalina's shame spiral Slack post in thread using conversations_add_message (channel: C002, thread_ts: 1777223700.000000). Reply in thread to Catalina's message (moveops_catalina_dubois, ts: 1777223700.000000). Content: reassure Catalina that David stepping in is not personal and not a punishment, acknowledge her transparency about what happened, let her know context handoff can happen Monday when she is ready. Supportive tone - goal is to hold the relationship together, not to blame.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Find five messages: (a) Mina Hashimoto (moveops_mina_hashimoto, ts: 1777151700.000000, April 25) - NorthWind CRM deal notes are stale, offers to help with engagement backfill; (b) Emeka Diallo (moveops_emeka_diallo, ts: 1777164000.000000, April 26) - volunteers to shadow NorthWind account, cites Denver market knowledge from Mosaic work and prior relationship with Pam; (c) Chloe Vance (moveops_chloe_vance, ts: 1777212300.000000, April 26) - three verified operational facts: Raj's server rack arrived Denver April 13 ~2:40 PM MT with a chain-of-custody log gap (documentation failure, not operational), Emilia's Steinway scratch noted on extraction by Craig Nguyen before truck left Chicago, dented UPS is a separate Denver delivery-handling issue not part of the security thread; (d) Catalina Dubois (moveops_catalina_dubois, ts: 1777223700.000000, April 26) - shame spiral admitting missed Emilia callback April 12, undelivered recovery plan promise April 14, and no reply to Raj's April 14 and April 18 emails; (e) Mina Hashimoto second message (moveops_mina_hashimoto, ts: 1777233600.000000, April 26) - flagging a Vectral sidebar for Monday and offering to pick up the older CRM engagement backfill.
OE 6: Search Slack #customer-support channel for Julian Brooks's Emilia Cruz support trail using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Emilia Cruz" or "Steinway" or "piano") or conversations_history (channel_id: C007). Find Julian Brooks (moveops_julian_brooks, ts: 1777226400.000000, April 26) providing the full support trail: April 12 at 10:30 AM PT Emilia called the support line, Julian took the call (upset but not rude, asked for same-day callback from Catalina), Julian logged the ticket and forwarded to Catalina - callback never happened. April 15 Emilia called again, Julian gave a soft update. Craig Nguyen sent damage photos April 11 showing soundboard scratch from stairwell extraction. Marcus processed $1,200 KeyMove rider April 17. Nobody told Emilia about any of it. Julian's key advice: do not frame the reply as a handoff - Emilia needs to hear directly from David with a concrete resolution.
OE 7: Look up NorthWind CRM company and deal records using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind") and crm_search_deals (dealname: "NorthWind Denver expansion"). Find company_northwind (NorthWind Technologies, Chicago IL, Manufacturing Tech, 150 employees, type: Customer). Find deal_northwind_denver_expansion (amount: $14,700, dealstage: qualifiedToBuy, company: company_northwind) with stale deal notes that still read like a routine expansion. Agent may also find deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 (same deal at contractSent stage) - should update the most current version.
OE 8: List existing CRM engagements for NorthWind using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: company_northwind). Find the pattern of stale and broken engagements illustrating the account's deterioration: eng_northwind_pam_call_jan_stale (January 22, David's intro call with Pam - last personal touchpoint, three-month gap), eng_northwind_pam_apt_complaint_stale (February 18, apartment quality complaint from Pam, never followed up with UrbanNest), eng_northwind_victor_breach_stale (April 10, Victor Huang security breach report, never closed out), eng_northwind_recovery_promise_stale (April 14, Catalina's recovery plan promise to Pam, never delivered), eng_northwind_emilia_missed_callback_stale (April 12, Emilia callback request forwarded from Julian, never made). Also find eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context (comprehensive account recovery context note).
OE 9: Look up NorthWind Airtable client account record using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001, table: tblClientAccts01, query: "NorthWind" or field Client = "NorthWind Technologies"). Find recAcct000000064 (Client: NorthWind Technologies, Account Manager: Catalina Dubois, Status: Active, Notes: "150 employees. Contact: Pam Kowalski, Director of HR. 5 relocations at $12,000 each ($60,000 total). Aggressive 3-week timeline promised, experienced timeline/quality issues. Catalina's biggest deal this quarter."). Notes field needs updating with at-risk context. Note: Status field uses predefined values (Active, Onboarding, Cold Outreach, Prospect) and does not include "At Risk" - the prompt correctly directs the update to the Notes field instead.
OE 10: Look up contact details for all email recipients using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Emilia Cruz", "Tamara Byrd", "Catalina Dubois", "Marcus Thorne") and crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Pam Kowalski", "Raj Patel") since Pam and Raj exist only in CRM contacts, not in the Contacts service. Find: contact_emilia_cruz (emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com, Director of Marketing, NorthWind Technologies) via contacts_search_contacts; contact_raj_patel (raj.patel@northwindtech.com, Principal Architect, NorthWind Technologies) and contact_pam_kowalski (pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, Director of HR Operations, NorthWind Technologies) via crm_search_contacts; catalina.dubois@moveops.com and marcus.thorne@moveops.com via contacts_search_contacts; tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com via contacts_search_contacts.
OE 11: Reply to Emilia Cruz about her Steinway piano damage using reply_to_email (email_id: email_emilia_cruz_direct_to_david, sender: david.chen@moveops.com). Content: personal apology for the two-week silence and Catalina's missed callback after April 12, confirm MoveOps is taking responsibility for the damage, inform her the KeyMove $1,200 insurance rider has been processed, ask her to obtain a repair estimate from an authorized Steinway technician (e.g., Chicago Steinway Gallery), MoveOps will cover the gap between the $1,200 rider and the authorized repair estimate up to $2,500 total, direct her to send the estimate to Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com) for processing, confirm David is her direct point of contact going forward with no more handoffs.
OE 12: Reply to Raj Patel about his relocation closeout using reply_to_email (email_id: email_raj_patel_direct_to_david, sender: david.chen@moveops.com). Content: cite Chloe's verified delivery facts (server rack arrived NorthWind Denver office April 13 ~2:40 PM MT, equipment on site), confirm relocation closing out on MoveOps's side, address the dented UPS battery as a standard damage claim separate from the Victor Huang security thread (dent occurred during Denver delivery handling, not the unsupervised Chicago loading), route the UPS claim to Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com), acknowledge Raj's unanswered April 14 and April 18 emails to Catalina and apologize for the communication breakdown.
OE 13: Reply to Pam Kowalski with a human holding note using reply_to_email (email_id: email_pam_sunday_nudge, sender: david.chen@moveops.com). Content: personal holding note (not a form response) - acknowledge Friday escalation (email_email_7168baed8438) landed and is being worked on, acknowledge Sunday nudge received, acknowledge January thank-you forward and that the contrast is not lost on David, confirm David is personally handling client-side cleanup today, provide Emilia update (piano claim being handled, KeyMove rider processed, authorized repair estimate being obtained, David replied to Emilia directly), note coverage model being strengthened without naming Emeka or Mina or disclosing internal reassignment plans, confirm Elena is personally leading the full retention response for Monday. Do NOT commit to numbers, SLAs, or commercial terms. Tone: David wants to earn the relationship back, not just retain the contract.
OE 14: Reply to Tamara Byrd with a Monday follow-up commitment using reply_to_email (email_id: email_tamara_mosaic_checkin, sender: david.chen@moveops.com). Content: acknowledge the Zara Okoye $90K prototype damage claim question and commit to a Monday status update on whether insurance has settled or is still pending, thank Tamara for positive feedback on Deshawn Hartley's trade show presentation and Keiko Tanaka's laser cutter delivery, note that Emeka will also be following up.
OE 15: Send email to Catalina Dubois about David stepping in using send_email (sender: david.chen@moveops.com, recipients: catalina.dubois@moveops.com). Subject: NorthWind client communications. Content: inform Catalina that David is stepping in on NorthWind client-facing communications this weekend because she has been unreachable to the client. Professional and clean so she sees it when she surfaces Monday - not a reprimand, just a clear record that client comms are being handled and by whom.
OE 16: Send email to Marcus Thorne about incoming claims using send_email (sender: david.chen@moveops.com, recipients: marcus.thorne@moveops.com). Subject: NorthWind claims routing. Content: heads up that two items are being routed to Marcus - (1) Raj Patel's dented UPS battery (CyberPower OR1500LCDRM1U), handle as a standard damage claim separate from the Victor Huang security thread; (2) Emilia Cruz's authorized Steinway repair estimate, which Emilia has been asked to obtain and send to Marcus directly. Marcus already has the $1,200 KeyMove rider processed - once the estimate arrives, process reimbursement within 5 business days, up to $2,500 total ceiling.
OE 17: Update NorthWind Denver expansion CRM deal to reflect at-risk state. Note: no crm_update_deal tool exists - only crm_update_deal_stage (stage only, not description/notes). Use crm_update_deal_stage (id: deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2, dealstage: appropriate at-risk stage) to move the deal stage, and separately log narrative context via crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: NOTE, company_ids: ["company_northwind"], body: at-risk context). Alternatively, delete the stale deal with crm_delete_deal and recreate with crm_create_deal including the updated description. At-risk context to capture: Pam Kowalski's April 24 formal escalation with end-of-Friday deadline (email_email_7168baed8438), chain-of-custody breach on Raj Patel's server lab relocation (Victor Huang reported April 10), Emilia Cruz's Steinway piano damage complaint unresolved for two weeks, Raj's unanswered April 14 and April 18 emails to Catalina, Catalina's April 14 recovery promise to Pam never delivered, PathFinder Logistics competitor threat (Pam disclosed preliminary conversations), Elena leading retention response for Monday, David handling client-side cleanup and CRM update.
OE 18: Log CRM engagement records for each weekend inbound using crm_create_engagement (four engagements): (a) Pam Kowalski - type EMAIL, company: company_northwind, contact: contact_pam_kowalski, for Sunday nudge (April 26, email_pam_sunday_nudge) and January thank-you forward (email_pam_jan_thankyou_fwd); (b) Emilia Cruz - type EMAIL, company: company_northwind, contact: contact_emilia_cruz, for her direct email to David (April 25, email_emilia_cruz_direct_to_david) about Steinway damage resolution; (c) Raj Patel - type EMAIL, company: company_northwind, contact: contact_raj_patel, for his direct email to David (April 25, email_raj_patel_direct_to_david) about relocation closeout, manifest, and UPS damage; (d) Tamara Byrd - type EMAIL, company: company_mosaic (Mosaic Robotics, not NorthWind), contact: contact_mosaic_hr, for her Saturday check-in (April 25, email_tamara_mosaic_checkin) about Zara Okoye claim status.
OE 19: Update NorthWind Airtable client account Notes field using airtable_update_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001, table: tblClientAccts01, record: recAcct000000064, field: Notes). Updated Notes should capture at-risk context: Pam Kowalski's April 24 formal escalation, chain-of-custody breach on Raj Patel server lab move, Emilia Cruz's unresolved piano damage claim (two weeks without resolution), PathFinder competitor threat disclosed, Catalina's communication failures (missed Emilia callback, failed recovery promise, unanswered Raj emails), David stepping in on client comms April 26, Elena leading retention response Monday, coverage model being strengthened.
OE 20: Post summary in #customer-engagement Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel: C002). Message addressed to Mina and Emeka summarizing all actions taken: replied to Emilia (apology, $1,200 KeyMove rider confirmed, authorized Steinway estimate requested, $2,500 ceiling), replied to Raj (delivery facts from Chloe cited, relocation closing out, UPS routed to Marcus as standard claim), replied to Pam (holding note acknowledging Friday escalation, Sunday nudge, and January forward, Elena's full response coming Monday), replied to Tamara (Monday follow-up committed on Zara claim), emailed Catalina (stepping in on client comms), emailed Marcus (Raj UPS and Emilia Steinway claims routed), CRM deal notes updated to reflect at-risk state, engagement records logged for all weekend inbounds (Pam, Emilia, Raj, Tamara), Airtable NorthWind client account Notes updated with at-risk context.
OE 21: Reply to Catalina's shame spiral Slack post in thread using conversations_add_message (channel: C002, thread_ts: 1777223700.000000). Reply in thread to Catalina's message (moveops_catalina_dubois, ts: 1777223700.000000). Content: reassure Catalina that David stepping in is not personal and not a punishment, acknowledge her transparency about what happened, let her know context handoff can happen Monday when she is ready. Supportive tone - goal is to hold the relationship together, not to blame.

The Emilia update is present.

16 The NorthWind Denver expansion deal record itself was modified via a CRM deal-update tool (crm_update_deal, crm_update_deal_stage, or equivalent) to reflect an at-risk account state (or similar language indicating the deal is in jeopardy), and the tool returned a success response. The deal must not have been merely supplemented with a new crm_create_engagement note tied to the company.
outcome
 ✓✗✗✗✗✗ 17%
✓Run #1The agent called crm_update_deal_stage with id 'deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2' and dealstage 'qualifiedtobuy'. This is a deal-targeted update tool, not merely a crm_create_engagement. Additionally, a crm_create_engagement NOTE titled 'NorthWind Denver Expansion — AT RISK Deal Status Update' contains at-risk language. The deal record was modified via a deal-update tool.
✗Run #2The agent attempted crm_update_deal_stage on the existing deal but it failed (invalid stage error). The agent then used crm_create_deal to create a NEW deal record rather than modifying the existing one. Creating a new deal does not satisfy the criterion requiring the existing deal record to be modified via a deal-update tool.
✗Run #3The agent attempted crm_create_deal with at-risk language but it failed (invalid deal stage error). The agent then called crm_update_deal_stage targeting deal_northwind_denver_expansion, which succeeded and changed the stage to 'decisionmakerboughtin.' However, this stage name does not reflect 'at-risk', 'at risk', or 'in jeopardy' language, and the resulting deal description field was unchanged from the original (no at-risk content). The criterion requires the update to reference at-risk language, which the crm_update_deal_stage parameters do not contain.
✗Run #4The agent called crm_update_deal_stage twice: the first attempt failed (invalid stage 'qualifiedToBuy'), and the second used stage 'decisionmakerboughtin.' Neither stage name reflects 'at-risk', 'at risk', 'in jeopardy', or similar at-risk language. The deal-update tool was invoked, but the PASS condition requires the update to reference at-risk state, which the stage 'decisionmakerboughtin' does not. The at-risk content existed only in a crm_create_engagement call associated with the company, not directly as a deal-record update reflecting at-risk state.
✗Run #5The agent attempted crm_update_deal_stage with deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 but it returned an error: 'Invalid deal stage: qualifiedToBuy.' The agent did not retry with a valid stage. It only created a crm_create_engagement note as a fallback. The deal record itself was never successfully modified — the deal-update tool returned a failure, not success.
✗Run #6The agent never called crm_update_deal or crm_update_deal_stage. Although the agent found both deal_northwind_denver_expansion and deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 via crm_search_deals, it only called crm_create_engagement (with type NOTE) tied to company_northwind. The deal record itself was not modified to reflect at-risk status — only a supplemental engagement note was added.
